document.name: Children's Museum of Manhattan (CMOM)
document.address: The Tisch Building, 212 West 83rd Street, New York, NY 10024
document.shortDescription: Upper West Side children’s museum (Tue–Sun, holiday Mondays) with interactive exhibits and daily programs designed especially for ages 0–6.
document.longDescription:
Children’s Museum of Manhattan (CMOM) is an Upper West Side museum in The Tisch Building at 212 West 83rd Street (between Broadway and Amsterdam). The museum states it is designed especially for children ages 0–6, with interactive exhibits and daily public programs included with admission.

Public hours from the official visit page: 10 a.m.–5 p.m. Tuesday–Sunday and on holiday Mondays.

Admission (visit page pricing table): online purchase $17 adult, $17 child, free for infants under 1; walk-up $18 adult, $18 child; discounted senior and accessibility rates listed on the same page. Online purchase saves $1 vs at-door pricing per the visit page.

Transit notes from CMOM: nearest subways include the 1 to 79th/86th and B/C to 81st/86th; several crosstown and avenue buses are listed on the visit page.

Visitor phone on visit page: 212-721-1223. Food is not allowed inside; stroller check is available and strollers are not permitted in exhibit areas per the visit page.

Sources: https://cmom.org/ https://cmom.org/visit/
document.announcementTitle: Save $1 when you buy tickets online
document.announcementDescription: CMOM lists lower online ticket prices than walk-up on its visit page — book before you go.
